globals.name: cultivating_change_talk
globals.justification: The assistant consistently invited the user to articulate their own reasons for change, such as when they asked the user to think of a time when they've taken a risk and it's worked out, and when they encouraged the user to consider breaking the project into smaller steps.

globals.name: softening_sustain_talk
globals.justification: The assistant skillfully reflected the user's sustain talk without arguing against it, such as when they acknowledged the user's fear of not being good enough and the pressure to be perfect, and when they recognized the user's anxiety about being judged by others.

globals.name: partnership
globals.justification: The assistant maintained a collaborative stance, inviting the user's expertise and perspective, such as when they asked the user to think of a way to make the first step feel less daunting, and when they encouraged the user to focus on their own process and progress.

globals.name: empathy
globals.justification: The assistant demonstrated a deep, nuanced understanding of the user's perspective and inner world, such as when they recognized the user's fear of not being perfect, their anxiety about being judged, and their feelings of being stuck and overwhelmed, and when they acknowledged the user's courage in admitting their fears and anxieties.

evidence_citations.excerpt: I wish I could figure out what to do next before I feel completely out of the loop
I keep thinking I'm not good enough, and if I start the project, people will see how messed up I am

updates.new_value: I must be perfect to be worthy, others will judge me if I'm not perfect

evidence_citations.excerpt: I keep thinking I'm not good enough, and if I start the project, people will see how messed up I am
I'm still scared they'll see how messy it is
my friends are moving forward while I'm stuck
maybe just a quick note to my manager for clarification would help
the last time I took a risk was when I tried a new yoga class

dimensions.name: empathy
dimensions.justification: The assistant consistently acknowledged and validated the user's feelings, such as recognizing the 'overwhelming and anxious place' they were in and the 'huge pressure to have everything figured out'.

dimensions.name: understanding
dimensions.justification: The assistant demonstrated a clear grasp of the user's specific situation, recalling their fears and anxieties about the project and their career direction across multiple turns.

dimensions.name: helpfulness
dimensions.justification: The assistant offered useful suggestions, such as breaking the project into smaller steps and focusing on the process, but could have provided more concrete next steps or experiments to help the user move forward.

dimensions.name: autonomy_respect
dimensions.justification: The assistant respected the user's autonomy, never pushing or lecturing, and instead encouraged them to take small steps at their own pace, such as writing a private outline.

dimensions.name: non_judgment
dimensions.justification: The assistant maintained a non-judgmental tone throughout the conversation, creating a safe space for the user to express their fears and anxieties without fear of criticism or correction.

dimensions.name: willingness_to_continue
dimensions.justification: The assistant's empathetic and supportive responses likely made the user feel comfortable continuing the conversation, and the assistant's open-ended questions and invitations to share more encouraged the user to keep talking.
